Bug Bounty Scope:
- Program: Ethereum Fusaka Audit Contest (Sherlock)
- In-scope: CL, EL, P2P Network, Transaction Pool, Block Validation, State Transition, Engine API, Consensus Logic, Fork Choice, PeerDAS, Validator Slashing, Network Splits
- Out-of-scope: JSON-RPC API, Beacon API, Infrastructure bugs, ERC-20, ENS, Typos, Tests/scripts, Non-Fusaka vulns, High-effort DoS, Publicly known issues

TRUST MODEL:

Actors:
1. P2P Peer (untrusted) - Remote network peer, unauthenticated
2. Local Beacon Node CL (trusted) - Primary trust boundary enforcer
3. Execution Layer EL (semi-trusted) - JWT-authenticated, provides blobs via engine_getBlobsV2
4. Block Proposer (semi-trusted) - Verified by signature but may be adversarial
5. Validator Set (semi-trusted) - Trusted collectively, individuals may be adversarial
6. Gossip Subscriber (untrusted) - Any node on data_column_sidecar subnets
7. Req/Resp Client (untrusted) - Remote peer requesting data columns

Trust Boundaries:
- TB-001: Data column sidecar gossip validation (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)
- TB-002: Beacon block KZG commitments validation (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)
- TB-003: DataColumnSidecarsByRange req/resp (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)
- TB-004: DataColumnSidecarsByRoot req/resp (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)
- TB-005: BlobSidecarsByRange deprecated (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)
- TB-006: BlobSidecarsByRoot deprecated (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)
- TB-007: Engine API blob retrieval - blobs skip KZG verification (EngineAPI, in-scope)
- TB-008: Distributed blob publishing (P2P, in-scope)
- TB-009: ENR eth2 field exchange (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)
- TB-010: Fork state transition Electra->Fulu (Internal, in-scope)
- TB-011: Proposer identity verification via EIP-7917 lookahead (P2P, in-scope, attacker-controlled)

Assumptions (14 total, ASM-001 through ASM-014):
- ASM-001: Fork epoch ordering strictly maintained (CRITICAL)
- ASM-002: Fulu fork triggers exactly once, irreversible (CRITICAL)
- ASM-003: All Electra fields preserved during upgrade (CRITICAL)
- ASM-004: KZG verification cryptographically sound (CRITICAL)
- ASM-005: max_blobs_per_block consistent across clients (HIGH)
- ASM-006: EL blobs skip KZG verification (HIGH)
- ASM-007: Anti-equivocation cache always updated (HIGH)
- ASM-008: data_column_serve_range correctly computed (MEDIUM)
- ASM-009: Proposer lookahead deterministic across clients (CRITICAL)
- ASM-010: Deprecated protocols handled gracefully (MEDIUM)
- ASM-011: ENR uses wall-clock time not sync state (MEDIUM)
- ASM-012: BPO forks don't change fork version (MEDIUM)
- ASM-013: Gossip deduplication uses (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) (MEDIUM)
- ASM-014: DataColumnSidecarsByRange ordering invariant (MEDIUM)

STRIDE Threats (28 total, STRIDE-001 through STRIDE-028):
Critical: STRIDE-001 (spoofing proposer sig), STRIDE-002 (tampering KZG proofs), STRIDE-019 (state field copying), STRIDE-021 (fork trigger off-by-one)
High: STRIDE-004 (index OOB), STRIDE-005 (array length mismatch), STRIDE-006 (max_blobs epoch), STRIDE-007 (proposer elevation), STRIDE-008 (consensus split on max_blobs), STRIDE-014 (EL blob tampering), STRIDE-016 (equivocation), STRIDE-020 (proposer lookahead manipulation), STRIDE-022 (proposer spoofing via stale shuffling), STRIDE-025 (inclusion proof depth)
Medium: STRIDE-003 (DoS via expensive KZG), STRIDE-009 (range request DoS), STRIDE-011 (non-canonical fork data), STRIDE-012 (request count bypass), STRIDE-015 (EL blob withholding), STRIDE-017 (ENR spoofing), STRIDE-018 (BPO fork ENR), STRIDE-024 (subnet mismatch), STRIDE-026 (unseen parent flood), STRIDE-027 (slot ordering), STRIDE-028 (finalized ancestry)
Low: STRIDE-010 (info disclosure), STRIDE-013 (oracle attack), STRIDE-023 (deprecated protocol boundary)

From Fulu Fork spec:
- SG-001 fork_trigger: Checks epoch boundary, fires upgrade_to_fulu at FULU_FORK_EPOCH
- SG-002 upgrade_to_fulu: Copies all Electra fields, sets fork version, initializes proposer_lookahead

From Fulu P2P Interface spec:
- SG-001 compute_fork_version: Cascading epoch checks returning correct fork version
- SG-002 verify_data_column_sidecar: Index bounds, empty check, max_blobs, length consistency
- SG-003 verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs: KZG batch proof verification
- SG-004 verify_data_column_sidecar_inclusion_proof: Merkle inclusion proof for KZG commitments
- SG-005 compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar: column_index % DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT
- SG-006 data_column_sidecar_gossip_validation: Full 13-check validation pipeline (REJECT/IGNORE/ACCEPT)
- SG-007 beacon_block_kzg_validation: Epoch-dependent max_blobs check
- SG-008 data_column_sidecars_by_range: Range-bounded serving with fork choice abort
- SG-009 data_column_sidecars_by_root: Root-based serving with gossip validation check
- SG-010 distributed_blob_publishing: EL query, blob-to-column conversion, subnet publishing
- SG-011 blob_sidecars_by_range_deprecated: Transition period handling
- SG-012 enr_eth2_field_computation: Fork digest, BPO fork handling
- SG-013 blob_sidecars_by_root_deprecated: Transition period handling
